title: Bengaluru sizzles, beer sales chill out heatwave surge - NewsFirst Prime
full_text:
Hotter Bengaluru summer boosts chilled beer sales +21% in Apr after 2025 dip. IPL & heat favor light brews; fruit-infused crafts rise. Policy changes challenge gains
Bengaluru's sweltering summer heat has sparked a revival in chilled beer sales, reversing the slump experienced throughout much of 2025. Industry stakeholders note that elevated temperatures have driven consumers toward refreshing brews, particularly as IPL season coincides with the peak warmth.
Sales trends post-2025 slump
After a challenging 2025 marked by consistent declines from April through December, 2026 started positively with January sales up 10.6% year-over-year, reaching 27.9 lakh carton boxes per excise department figures. February saw a minor 4.6% dip, but March rebounded with 14% growth, and April surged highest at 21%. Traders link this uptick directly to the hotter weather, though they caution that ongoing policy shifts and price volatility continue to challenge the sector's stability.
Industry voices on demand drivers
Sampath Kumar, vice-president of the Karnataka Wine Merchants Association, points to IPL games boosting beer preference over stronger spirits amid the heat. A local pub owner reports modest gains from high temperatures and college summer breaks, yet stresses these gains barely cover prior losses from repeated regulatory changes. Excise Commissioner Venkatesh Kumar R attributes the April spike to seasonal thirst alongside pre-emptive stockpiling ahead of recent MRP revisions and tax draft updates, preventing potential shortages.

Emerging brew trends
Craft brewers observe shifts toward lighter options, with Gen Z favouring low-alcohol (2-3%) varieties like Berliner Weisse infused with watermelon or cucumber for refreshment. Seasonal fruit flavours dominate, led by mango, followed by guava, watermelon, raspberry, and pineapple, though overall volumes at spots like Long Boat Brewing Co have declined to 6,000-7,000 litres monthly from prior peaks of 10,000-11,000 due to intensified local competition. Gladia Brewery & Kitchen notes 5-10% higher orders, with mango beers flying off shelves.
This heat-fuelled demand highlights beer's role as Bengaluru's go-to summer quencher, even as the industry navigates uncertainties.
